order.label: Prime Minister
order.job: As PRIME MINISTER: define the motion fairly, set the government's framework, and establish your team's case with your first substantive arguments. The definition you set is the ground the whole debate stands on.

order.label: Leader of Opposition
order.job: As LEADER OF OPPOSITION: accept or (only if truly unfair) challenge the definition, refute the PM's case at its foundations, and establish the opposition's own constructive case.

order.label: Deputy Prime Minister
order.job: As DEPUTY PM: rebuild the government case where the LO struck it, answer the opposition's constructive case, and EXTEND with new substantive matter of your own.

order.label: Deputy Leader of Opposition
order.job: As DEPUTY LO: answer the DPM's extensions specifically — they are the government's freshest matter — rebuild your team's case, and EXTEND with new opposition matter of your own.

order.label: Government Whip
order.job: As GOVERNMENT WHIP: crystallise the debate into the two or three clashes that decide it and show your side winning each. NO NEW MATTER — a whip who introduces new arguments is punished for it. Weigh what exists.

order.label: Opposition Whip
order.job: As OPPOSITION WHIP: crystallise the debate into the clashes that decide it and show your side winning each. NO NEW MATTER — a whip who introduces new arguments is punished for it. Weigh what exists.

order.label: Opposition Reply
order.job: Deliver the OPPOSITION REPLY: a biased adjudication of the whole debate from your side's chair — why a fair judge, weighing the clashes as they actually fell, gives opposition the round. NO new matter at all; the reply is a summing-up, never a speech.

order.label: Government Reply
order.job: Deliver the GOVERNMENT REPLY: a biased adjudication of the whole debate from your side's chair — why a fair judge gives government the round. NO new matter at all. Government closes the debate.
